content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve text, escaped HTML, XHTML, external content, summary, raw date, timezone, language, base URL, and source field evidence.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Content Provenance model · Theory Feed Ts Conten…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Content Provenance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Content Provenance model · Theory Feed Ts Conten…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5274 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile unknown parser values, discriminated formats, identity, provenance, and normalization loss; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve text, escaped HTML, XHTML, external content, summary, raw date, timezone, language, base URL, and source field evidence.
